texto: Once your baby arrives, the world is no more the same than you are. Because from our very bodies we add to the collective human destiny. Our deepest urge is always toward life, to wholeness and well being.
explicacao: Citação de Claire Fontaine sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Once your baby arrives, the world is no more the same than you are. frase_original: Once your baby arrives, the world is no more the same than you are. Because from our very bodies we add to the collective human destiny. Our deepest urge is always toward life, to wholeness and well being.

texto: What was the payoff? It obviously kept me in my cozy zone of being in control, being a good mother, with a good daughter. Most of all, I realize, is that it allowed me to maintain the lie that she was healed, that Nick hadn't permanently damaged her, that I'd truly saved her. Because if I did, if there was no lasting residue of him, it meant that the denial that kept me in the marriage long enough for him to hurt her didn't help create the situation she's in now. The person who I worked hardest to keep safe seems to have been me.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Claire Fontaine que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “What was the payoff? It obviously kept me in my cozy zone of being in control, being a good mothe…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, denial, self-awareness, com foco em casamento e trabalho.

texto: Travel empties out everything you’ve into the box called your life, all the things you accumulate to tell you who you are
